chief_complaint: A 44-year-old woman presented with a large pulmonary infarction.
history: Sarcoidosis is a systemic, granulomatous disorder commonly affecting the lungs. The patient had pulmonary sarcoidosis with a large pulmonary infarction attributed to arterial stenosis resulting from sarcoidosis-associated fibrosing mediastinitis and compressive mediastinal adenopathy. The patient was treated with an extended course of prednisone and subsequently was transitioned to azathioprine.
exam: Vital signs and physical examination findings are not provided.
labs: ECG, bloods, imaging, and other tests results are not provided.
correct_diagnosis: Arterial stenosis from sarcoidosis-associated adenopathy and fibrosing mediastinitis leading to pulmonary infarction
differentials: Arterial stenosis from sarcoidosis-associated adenopathy and fibrosing mediastinitis leading to pulmonary infarction
Pulmonary infarction due to pulmonary artery thrombosis
correct_ranking: Arterial stenosis from sarcoidosis-associated adenopathy and fibrosing mediastinitis leading to pulmonary infarction
Pulmonary infarction due to pulmonary artery thrombosis
